_comment_top: R6 Pillar 6b / D-C-05 / task 054 — seed row for the SendWorkspaceArtifactHandler chat tool. Surfaces a single LLM-visible 'send_workspace_artifact(widgetType, title, widgetData, matterId?)' function that dispatches a finished agent artifact to the user's workspace pane as a new tab via IWorkspaceStateService.UpsertTabAsync.
_comment_routing: Runtime invocation routes to the C# class via sprk_handlerclass = 'SendWorkspaceArtifactHandler' (= nameof handler class). Auto-discovered via ToolFrameworkExtensions.AddToolHandlersFromAssembly (ADR-010) — ZERO new top-level DI line.
_comment_placement_boundary: ADR-013 binding: the handler injects IWorkspaceStateService directly (BFF-internal workspace plumbing) — NOT through a PublicContracts facade. This mirrors task 053's SprkChatAgentFactory.ResolveTools system-prompt block which also reads IWorkspaceStateService directly from the per-turn scope. Workspace state is a chat-session state primitive owned by Pillar 6a, not an AI capability — facade indirection would add no boundary value.
_comment_security: sprk_requiredcapability = null (intentional). Sending an artifact to the workspace pane is a default user affordance — every chat session, standalone or playbook-bound, can dispatch artifacts. The 4-variant widgetType enum (Summary | DocumentViewer | Dashboard | Table) is the authorization surface — there is no 'free text widget' escape hatch, so the LLM cannot smuggle arbitrary payloads into the workspace outside the closed union.
_comment_tenant_isolation: ADR-014 binding: the chat session's TenantId is forwarded into the tab's TenantId + the underlying Redis key + Cosmos partition key. Cross-tenant dispatch is structurally impossible — WorkspaceStateService.UpsertTabAsync enforces a tenant match between the arg tenantId and the WorkspaceTab.TenantId, throwing InvalidOperationException on mismatch (caught by the handler + surfaced as ValidationFailed).
_comment_adr015: ADR-015 data governance: telemetry logs handler name + decision GUID + tabId GUID + widgetType discriminator + matter scope present/absent + title LENGTH + duration ONLY. NEVER the LLM-supplied widgetData body content; NEVER the title text; NEVER the user's chat message. The tab's SourceProvenance.CreatedBy is the deterministic 'agent:{chatSessionId}' sentinel — never user message text.
_comment_description_loadbearing: sprk_description is LOAD-BEARING per NFR-12: the LLM uses the description text to decide WHEN to invoke this tool. The phrasing emphasizes (1) the four widget archetypes the LLM can produce, (2) the conversational-primacy invariant (chat reply stays conversational; artifact materializes on the tab strip), and (3) the user-pin-to-matter follow-up affordance. Pillar 9 (task 074) will refine the description with per-widget visible-state semantics if needed.
_comment_consumption: Read by scripts/Seed-TypedHandlers.ps1. Idempotent — script queries by sprk_handlerclass + sprk_toolcode with sprk_name 'SYS-%' filter; UPSERTs.
sprk_description: Send a finished artifact (Summary, DocumentViewer, Dashboard, or Table) to the user's workspace pane as a new tab. Use this when you have produced a structured result the user should be able to inspect, share, or pin to a matter — for example: a generated executive summary (Summary), a previewed contract or knowledge source (DocumentViewer), a portfolio overview (Dashboard), or a sortable result set (Table). The artifact materializes on the workspace tab strip; the chat reply remains conversational. Agent-created tabs default to visible-to-assistant so you can reference them in subsequent turns; the user can pin a tab to a matter via the workspace UI.
_comment_availableincontexts: 100000001 = Chat only. Workspace tab dispatch is a chat-surface affordance — playbook nodes write to sprk_analysisoutput + sprk_workingdocument, not the chat-session per-tenant workspace tab list. Future R7+ work MAY extend this to Both (100000002) if cross-context artifact dispatch becomes a coherent flow.

sprk_jsonschema.title: SendWorkspaceArtifactHandler Request
sprk_jsonschema.description: Argument shape for chat-driven invocation of the send_workspace_artifact tool. Constructs a WorkspaceTab and persists it via IWorkspaceStateService.UpsertTabAsync.

widgetType.description: Closed enum selecting the workspace tab variant. The 'widgetData.kind' field MUST equal this value.

title.description: Display title for the workspace tab — shown on the tab strip and in tooltips. Should be short and matter-relevant. NEVER raw user message text — author a concise headline that summarizes the artifact.

kind.description: Discriminator — MUST equal widgetType. Per-variant fields below are validated structurally by the handler via System.Text.Json polymorphism.

widgetData.description: Per-variant payload. Summary requires {body, tldr?}; DocumentViewer requires {documentId, filename, mimeType, sizeBytes, hasSelection?, selectionText?}; Dashboard requires {layoutId, dashboardName, lastViewedSection?}; Table requires {rowCount, filteredColumns[], selectedRows[], sortColumn?, sortDirection?, dataSourceId?}. Deterministic identifiers + display metadata only — ADR-015 binding: NEVER raw user message text.

matterId.description: Optional Dataverse sprk_matter GUID to anchor the tab to. When omitted, the chat session's MatterId is used; when neither is present the tab is anchored to a synthetic 'unattached' context (Guid.Empty) that the user can later pin to a real matter via the workspace UI.

matterName.description: Optional display name for the matter — shown in the tab tooltip when matterId is supplied. When omitted the GUID is used as the display name.

sprk_configuration._comment: No method-discriminator dispatch — this row exposes a single LLM-facing function. Empty object reserved for future per-row defaults (e.g., default widget-type override, per-tenant 'always-pin' policy).
